paper_title: Holocene Glacier Ice Cap (GIC) Variations in the Arctic

methods.method_name: Compilation of Holocene Lake-Based GIC Records
methods.description: This method involves compiling and analyzing published records of Holocene glacier ice cap (GIC) variations from Arctic regions using lake sediment data to infer past climate conditions.

steps.description: We compiled all published Holocene lake-based GIC records from the Arctic, which we define as land area above 58°N.
steps.searchable_summary: compile published Holocene lake-based GIC records from Arctic regions
outputs: compiled dataset of Arctic GIC records

steps.description: The records report local GIC fluctuations through the Holocene reconstructed via analysis of lacustrine sediments from downstream glacial lakes.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze lacustrine sediments to reconstruct GIC fluctuations

steps.description: Erosion at the ice-bed interface produces rock flour which is transported to downstream lakes via proglacial meltwater streams.
steps.searchable_summary: process sediment data to identify rock flour from erosion

steps.description: Intervals dominated by minerogenic, fine-grained sediment are interpreted as reflective of glacier presence, whereas intervals higher in organic material are interpreted as periods of reduced glacier size or absence.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret sediment composition to infer glacier presence or absence

steps.description: We interpret periods in which GICs are reported as smaller than present or absent altogether as indicative of summer temperatures warmer than present.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret GIC size data to infer past summer temperatures

steps.description: In total we compiled 66 lake-based records of Holocene GIC variations from seven regions.
steps.searchable_summary: compile 66 lake-based records of GIC variations from specified regions

steps.description: For each lake record we documented when GICs were smaller than present or had melted away completely in the early-to-middle Holocene and when the lake subsequently became glacially influenced again in the middle-to-late Holocene.
steps.searchable_summary: document GIC size changes over Holocene periods
